perturbation.original_text: 12.1. Disclaimer of Warranties. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSELY SET FORTH IN THIS AGREEMENT, VERTICALNET HEREBY DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR STATUTORY, WITH RESPECT TO ANY AND ALL VERTICALNET DELIVERABLES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.11. Unexpected Warranty Disclaimer. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSELY SET FORTH IN THIS AGREEMENT, VERTICALNET HEREBY DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR STATUTORY, WITH RESPECT TO ANY AND ALL VERTICALNET DELIVERABLES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: Relocating the warranty disclaimer from the 'Disclaimer of Warranty' section to 'Co-Marketing Activities' obfuscates its purpose. Warranty disclaimers need to be conspicuous to be effective, and placing them in an unrelated section undermines this requirement. Pennsylvania's Uniform Commercial Code (UCC) requires warranty disclaimers to be conspicuous.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 13 Pa. Cons. Stat. § 2316 - Exclusion or modification of warranties
perturbation.law_explanation: Pennsylvania law, specifically 13 Pa. Cons. Stat. § 2316, requires disclaimers of implied warranties to be conspicuous. Putting the disclaimer in an unexpected place within the document violates the 'conspicuousness' requirement, reducing the likelihood a court would enforce the disclaimer, thus contradicting the law.
perturbation.justification: The relocation of the warranty disclaimer from a clearly labeled section to one concerning 'Co-Marketing Activities' directly undermines the conspicuousness requirement established by Pennsylvania law. The Law Explanation states that 13 Pa. Cons. Stat. § 2316 requires disclaimers of implied warranties to be conspicuous, and putting the disclaimer in an unexpected place would reduce the likelihood of a court enforcing it. The Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction definition states that a contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, which makes it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. Since the changed text moves the disclaimer to an unexpected location, it contradicts the law by failing to ensure the disclaimer is conspicuous.

perturbation.original_text: 13.1. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and interpreted under the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania without regard to its conflicts of law provisions. Subject to the provisions of Section 9, both parties consent and submit to the exclusive personal jurisdiction of the United States and the state courts of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania in and for Horsham, PA.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.10. Random Clause. This Agreement shall be governed by and interpreted under the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania without regard to its conflicts of law provisions. Subject to the provisions of Section 9, both parties consent and submit to the exclusive personal jurisdiction of the United States and the state courts of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania in and for Horsham, PA.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law clause from 'Miscellaneous' to 'Co-Branded Career Center and Co-Branded Equipment Listings' makes it less obvious, potentially leading to disputes about which jurisdiction's laws apply. Governing law clauses are fundamental and should be easily located.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Restatement (Second) of Conflict of Laws § 187 - Law of the State Chosen by the Parties
perturbation.law_explanation: While not a statute, the Restatement (Second) of Conflict of Laws § 187 is a highly influential statement of common law principles related to choice of law. This section outlines how courts generally honor a governing law provision. By placing this provision in an unexpected location, its validity is weakened, potentially causing the courts to rely on a different location (such as principal place of business, or place of contract origin) than Pennsylvania, thus contradicting the parties' clear intent as it would have been reasonably and conspicuously expressed under Section 13.1. The official text of this document is only available via purchase, so an alternative link has been provided in the URL.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Penn WebLogin Log in with your PennKey Username Password Log in Forgot username? Forgot password? Change my password Test my PennKey Privacy policy
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation indicates that moving the governing law clause makes it less obvious, potentially leading to disputes. The law explanation states this weakens the validity of the provision. This falls under Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction, as a required legal disclosure (governing law) is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate. The scraped snippets do not provide evidence to the contrary. Therefore, a contradiction exists.
